title: manihot grahamii
intro: Manihot grahamii, also known as Graham's cassava or Graham's yam, is a species of plant native to the Americas, specifically in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant's roots have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Manihot grahamii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are often dried, crushed, or made into a decoction for consumption, and are also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and wounds.
